name: Cronitor Cron Job and Heartbeat Monitoring API
what_it_does: Cron job, scheduled task, and background job monitoring platform with REST API — enabling developers to monitor whether scheduled jobs run on time, complete successfully, and trigger alerts when jobs are late, failed, or running too long, with a heartbeat ping API for simple health checks, real-time dashboards, and notification integrations.
use_cases: Monitoring cron jobs via Cronitor ping API for scheduled task reliability alerting
Implementing heartbeat health checks via Cronitor for background worker liveness monitoring
Tracking job execution time via Cronitor API for performance regression detection
Setting up SLO monitoring for scheduled tasks via Cronitor for operational compliance
Integrating Cronitor with CI/CD for deployment job monitoring and alerting
not_for: Application performance monitoring (use Datadog or New Relic for full APM)
Uptime monitoring for web services (use BetterUptime or Pingdom for URL monitoring)
Full incident management workflows (use PagerDuty for on-call management)
best_when: A development team running scheduled jobs (cron, Celery, Sidekiq, AWS Lambda scheduled events) needs simple but reliable monitoring — where Cronitor's ping API enables quick integration without complex agent setup.
avoid_when: You need full APM, web uptime monitoring, or complex incident management workflows.

auth.notes: API key via HTTP Basic auth (API key as username, blank password). Ping API: GET/POST https://cronitor.link/{monitorKey}?msg=... — no auth required (monitor key in URL). Management API: Authorization: Basic {base64(apiKey:)} header. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Webhooks for alert notifications. Python/Ruby/Go/JS/PHP/Java SDKs.

pricing.notes: US company. Founded 2015 by Andrew Culver and Dave Woodall. Bootstrapped profitable. Simple product philosophy (do one thing well). 10,000+ developers. Ping API is the core: one HTTP call from your job. Telemetry API for job execution tracking. Dashboard for visualizing job schedule and history. Notification channels: email, Slack, PagerDuty, OpsGenie, webhook. Cronitor CLI for easy shell script integration. SLO tracking for jobs. Popular with Ruby/Rails developers (Sidekiq, Clockwork integration). Python SDK widely used (Celery, APScheduler). Dead Man's Switch pattern. Cron expression support for schedule validation.

performance.rate_limits: Ping API: high throughput; Management API: rate limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with HTTP Basic auth. Ping API (no auth, monitor key in URL). Management API (Basic auth). Python/Ruby/Go/JS/PHP/Java SDKs. Webhooks. Free tier for testing. Great for monitoring agent-scheduled jobs. Simple, focused product.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Ping API calls fully idempotent for same monitor key. Monitor reads idempotent. Monitor creation idempotent with unique key. Status updates idempotent by monitor key.
known_agent_gotchas: Two distinct APIs: Ping API (no auth, for jobs) vs Management API (Basic auth, for config)
Ping API URL: https://cronitor.link/{monitorKey} — monitor key ≠ API key
Job lifecycle: /run (start) → /complete (success) or /fail (error) — must send both start and complete/fail
Monitor key is unique per job — create monitor in dashboard or via Management API first
Ping API has no auth — anyone with monitor key can send pings; don't log monitor keys publicly
Use SDKs for automatic run/complete/fail lifecycle tracking — manual pings miss timing data

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HTTP Basic auth (API key as username). Ping API uses monitor key (not auth key) — monitor key in URL path. No OAuth2 scope granularity. US data residency. Job execution data is non-sensitive (timestamps, success/fail). Monitor keys shouldn't be public. No formal compliance certifications.
